Raise TTT cosine LR floor from 0 to 0.4·ttt_lr so late val chunks (where the model has accumulated useful drift toward val distribution) keep adapting at meaningful LR instead of freezing.

Switch TTT inner SGD from heavy-ball to Nesterov-accelerated momentum (nesterov=True). Across ~58K inner steps spanning 305 chunks at constant per-chunk LR, the lookahead update should converge faster per step at zero memory/compute cost.

Layer-wise LR decay for TTT inner SGD: layer i gets 0.85^(10-i)·ttt_lr (5× total span across depth) — concentrate per-chunk adaptation on later blocks where distribution shift mostly manifests, keep early-layer token features stable. Outer cosine schedule, momentum, and step count unchanged.

Add weight_decay=1e-4 to TTT SGD optimizer to mildly regularize cumulative drift across ~58K inner steps over 305 chunks; lr×wd=5e-7 per step gives ~3% pull-toward-zero washing out idiosyncratic per-chunk gradient noise while leaving persistent val-distribution signal intact.

Sharper GPTQ calibration: Hessian damp factor 0.01→0.005 and lazy-batch block_size 128→32 — together push the post-quant weights closer to the FP optimum since both knobs trade calibration robustness for fidelity, an underexplored surface in this lineage (TTT-tweaks 005/006/007 all discarded).

Add PaLM-style z-loss aux (coef 1e-4 · mean(logsumexp(logits)^2)) to training/TTT cross-entropy: bounds the softmax partition function, tightening logit dynamic range so downstream GPTQ int6 quantization noise is relatively smaller per-token; orthogonal to the saturated TTT-tuning thread.

Raise EMA_DECAY from 0.9965 to 0.999 (3.5× longer half-life: ~198 → ~693 steps) so end-of-training EMA averages a substantive slice of the 72% linear warmdown phase rather than only its tail — extracts more noise-reduction across late low-LR fine-tuning.

Reset SGD momentum buffer at each TTT chunk boundary (optimizer.state.clear() per chunk). The single optimizer instance carries momentum across all 305 heterogeneous val-text chunks, so each chunk inherits stale ~10-step-filtered gradient state from prior chunks' (different-topic) tokens — clean per-chunk local momentum should give a more accurate descent direction while preserving the cumulative param drift exp_004 confirmed helps.

Freeze attention Q/K/V projections (c_q, c_k, c_v) during TTT in addition to tok_emb — the routing matrices shouldn't drift on chunk-local 32K windows; let attn.proj+MLP+norms absorb per-chunk feature-space adaptation (TENT/FFN-adaptation thesis, same structural-freeze axis as the historically strongest exp_002 move).

Split TTT SGD into two param groups by ndim — 1D per-channel gates (q_gain, attn_out_gate, attn_scale, mlp_scale) get 5× ttt_lr (0.025), 2D matrix params keep 1× (0.005). Apply eval-time temperature scaling T=1.05 to TTT score-first logits only (TTT training stays T=1.0): post-GPTQ-INT6 + score-first TTT can leave the softmax over-sharpened on chunk-local peaks, mild flattening recovers NLL on hard mid-tail tokens.

Set TTT inner-loop batch_seqs=1 (decoupled from scoring batch_seqs=32) so each chunk runs ~6 SGD steps (1 per minibatch × 2 minibatches × 3 epochs) instead of ~3 — doubles per-chunk adaptation drift via finer per-step granularity, extending exp_004's late-chunk-adaptation mechanism.

QuaRot-style Hadamard rotation of every non-embed weight's input axis before GPTQ (W' = W @ H, transformed Hessian H' = H_R^T @ H_orig @ H_R, un-rotated at dequant via deq @ H since normalized Sylvester Hadamard is symmetric-orthogonal): the orthogonal rotation maps each weight row to a near-Gaussian distribution (kurtosis ~3) by spreading outlier energy across input dims, redistributing the GPTQ column-correction bias away from input-aligned directions; row_std preserved so the int6 grid step is unchanged.

Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine decay (same endpoints: 1.0→min_lr=0.1), keeping LR higher for longer in mid-warmdown to give more gradient signal when depth-recurrence activates at frac≈0.35, then a steeper final convergence.

Reduce GPTQ error-compensation block_size from 128 to 32: smaller blocks mean more frequent cross-column error propagation within each Hinv update step, matching the multi_596 seed description (which specifies block_size=32) and the general GPTQ principle that finer granularity reduces quantization error.

ResFormer value residual (arxiv 2410.17897): thread block[0]'s V projection of x0 to all attention layers as a learnable residual (sigmoid-gated, init≈0.047), providing a direct embedding→V shortcut that alleviates attention concentration with zero new weight matrices and 11 tiny scalar parameters.

Focal loss (γ=2) during training: re-weight per-token CE by (1-pt)²/mean((1-pt)²) where pt=exp(-ce_token), computed via.detach() to avoid gradient through weights. Hard/uncertain tokens get up to ~80× more gradient than easy tokens. TTT adaptation uses standard CE (matching eval objective) via forward_logits + explicit CE. No new parameters; zero memory overhead beyond the detached per-token CE.

Key residual from block[0] alongside value residual: thread k_res = blocks[0].c_k(attn_norm(x0)*scale) to all attention layers, adding sigmoid(k_res_scale)*k_res to each layer's K before rms_norm. Extends ResFormer to also provide a direct x0→K shortcut, biasing attention patterns toward the initial embedding's key features. 11 new scalar parameters (k_res_scale per block), same forward-pass cost as v_res.

Polar Express Newton-Schulz (arxiv 2505.16932): replace fixed (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) applied uniformly across all 5 Muon NS steps with 5 per-step minimax-optimal coefficients from modded-nanogpt, which better approximate the polar factor by adapting to the current spectral range at each iteration.

Polar Express Newton-Schulz (arxiv 2505.16932): replace fixed uniform coefficients (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) with 5 per-step minimax-optimal coefficients, giving each of the 5 NS iterations its own optimal polynomial approximation to the sign function rather than re-using a single sub-optimal set — should improve Muon gradient orthogonalization quality at zero compute cost.

Polar Express Newton-Schulz (arxiv 2505.16932): replace fixed uniform coefficients (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) with 5 per-step minimax-optimal coefficients so each NS iteration uses the polynomial that minimizes worst-case distance from the polar factor at that convergence stage.

Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping from 0.01 to 0.005: halving the diagonal regularization reduces Hessian bias, allowing more accurate weight reconstruction during quantization (technique from PR #1318 context).

Bifurcated q_gain [H,2]: independent learnable temperature scales for RoPE dims (0–15, positional) vs NoPE dims (16–63, semantic), both initialized at 5.25 — allows the model to separately tune attention sharpness in positional vs semantic subspaces, the design the seed description intended but the codebase never implemented.

Four accumulated improvements over exp_081: (1) bifurcated q_gain [H,2] separating RoPE/NoPE attention temperature scales; (2) Polar Express per-step minimax-optimal NS coefficients (arxiv 2505.16932) replacing fixed (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315); (3) GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 reducing quantization bias; (4) Nesterov=True in TTT SGD for faster per-chunk lookahead convergence.

Polar Express per-step minimax-optimal NS coefficients (arxiv 2505.16932, verified vs Dao-AILab/gram-newton-schulz) replacing fixed (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315), plus GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 for less-biased quantization.

Per-layer TTT LR scaling (block i: lr_scale=0.5+i/10 ∈ [0.5,1.5], outer blocks adapt 3× faster) + Nesterov=True SGD: concentrates TTT budget on output-proximal layers where quantization errors most directly affect BPB, while Nesterov look-ahead reduces per-chunk oscillations (arXiv:2602.09719).

Polar Express per-step minimax-optimal NS coefficients (5 steps, arxiv 2505.16932) replacing fixed uniform (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) + GPTQ Hessian damping 0.005 (from 0.01): two improvements accumulated from failed infrastructure attempts after exp_081, now submitted clean without bifurcated q_gain.

Polyak TTT weight averaging (decay=0.998): before scoring each chunk (ci>0) swap in a float32 EMA of all past gradient-step weights, then restore gradient weights for training — smoother, less-overfit scoring weights without changing the training trajectory (adapted from PR #995).

Polyak TTT weight averaging (decay=0.998): before scoring each chunk (ci>0) swap in a float32 EMA of all past gradient-step weights, then restore gradient weights for training — smoother, less-overfit scoring weights without changing the training trajectory (adapted from PR #995).

Bundle of 4 accumulated improvements over exp_081: (1) Polar Express per-step NS coefficients (arxiv 2505.16932) replacing fixed (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315); (2) GPTQ Hessian damping halved 0.01→0.005 for less-biased quantization; (3) per-layer TTT LR scaling (0.5+i/(L-1) ∈ [0.5,1.5]); (4) Polyak TTT weight averaging (decay=0.998) using EMA of adapted weights for inference.

Bundle of 4 accumulated improvements over exp_081: (1) Polar Express per-step minimax-optimal NS coefficients (5 tuples, arxiv 2505.16932) replacing fixed (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) for better Muon orthogonalization; (2) GPTQ Hessian damping 0.01→0.005 reducing calibration bias; (3) per-layer TTT LR scaling (block i: 0.5+i/10 ∈ [0.5,1.5]); (4) Nesterov=True in TTT SGD — all previously infrastructure-blocked.

Bundle of 4 accumulated improvements over exp_081 (all infrastructure-failed in prior sessions): (1) Polar Express per-step minimax-optimal NS coefficients (steps=10→5, arxiv 2505.16932); (2) GPTQ Hessian damping halved 0.01→0.005 (less regularization bias, PR #1318); (3) per-layer TTT LR scaling (outer blocks lr×1.5, inner ×0.5, arXiv 2602.09719); (4) Nesterov=True in TTT SGD for lookahead convergence.

Bundle of 4 accumulated improvements over exp_081 (all infrastructure-failed in prior sessions): (1) Polar Express per-step minimax-optimal NS coefficients (arxiv 2505.16932), (2) per-layer TTT LR scaling 0.5→1.5 concentrating adaptation on outer blocks, (3) Nesterov=True in TTT SGD, (4) GPTQ Hessian damp 0.01→0.005.

Remove nesterov=True from TTT SGD (revert to heavy-ball momentum): exp_005 showed Nesterov added +0.000154 BPB on the same TTT axis; exp_146's bundle inadvertently included it, masking potential gains from Polar Express NS + per-layer LR scaling + GPTQ damp 0.005.

FreqGPTQ: boost top-100 most-frequent tokens' activations by sqrt(2) during GPTQ Hessian accumulation → 2× weight in H=X^TX for those tokens, biasing int6 quantization fidelity toward positions that dominate val_bpb at zero artifact cost (technique from PR #1721).

K residual: thread block[0]'s K projection (computed on x0) to all attention layers as a learnable residual (sigmoid-gated, init≈0.047), analogous to the V residual (exp_081: -0.001067 BPB). Anchors each layer's key representation to the original token embedding, preventing key drift in deep networks and providing direct embedding→K shortcut.

Polyak TTT weight averaging (decay=0.998): before scoring each chunk (ci>0), swap in float32 EMA of all prior adapted model weights; after scoring, restore current weights for adaptation; update EMA after each chunk's SGD steps. EMA smooths out chunk-specific overfitting in adapted weights so each chunk is scored with a more globally-representative model state.

Increase TTT SGD momentum from 0.9 to 0.95: strengthens cross-chunk momentum carryover (the mechanism behind exp_004/047/048's monotonic TTT gains), extending the effective adaptation memory from ~10-20 chunks to ~30-50 chunks with 3 SGD steps/chunk. With 305 total val chunks, higher momentum accumulates stronger directional drift toward the val distribution under the constant-LR regime established by exp_048.

Differential attention (DIFF Transformer style, arxiv 2410.05258): split Q/K/V each in half along head_dim (64→32+32), two FA3 calls per layer, subtract per-head learnable λ=sigmoid(diff_lambda, init=-3.0≈0.047) from first attention output; y=cat([y1-λ*y2, y2], dim=-1). Noise cancellation via differential attention maps; no parameter or artifact size increase beyond 11×8=88 trivial scalars.

Replace fixed leaky_relu(h, 0.5).square() with learnable per-layer activation slope: torch.where(h >= 0, h, sigmoid(act_slope) * h).square() where act_slope is a new nn.Parameter(0.0) per MLP block, allowing each layer to adapt its negative slope (sigmoid(0)=0.5=current default) toward relu² (slope→0) or away from it during training.

Revert harmful learnable MLP activation slope (exp_153 degraded +0.00136 BPB) and add z-loss regularization (1e-4*logsumexp(logits)^2) to training+TTT: z-loss constrains log-partition function scale, complementing existing logit_softcap, preventing overconfident predictions, and potentially improving GPTQ quantization via smaller logit outliers.

Reduce GPTQ block_size 128→64: smaller blocks give 2× more frequent Hessian-error corrections per matrix, improving quantization accuracy. exp_008 tried 128→32 (too aggressive) vs the old baseline; 64 is more moderate and should be near-optimal for our 512-dim matrices.

Bifurcated q_gain [H,2]: separate learnable temperature scales for RoPE dims (position-aware, first 16 of 64) vs NoPE dims (content-aware, remaining 48), allowing the model to independently optimize attention sharpness in each subspace. Initialization: both columns = 5.25 (same as current single q_gain).

K residual: thread block[0]'s K projection of x0 to all attention layers as a learnable additive residual (sigmoid-gated, init≈0.047), applied after K's RMSNorm — symmetric to V residual (exp_081, -0.001067 BPB). Provides embedding→K shortcuts so each layer's keys carry token-identity signal for induction-head patterns.

Increase EMA decay 0.9965→0.9985: broader SWA-like averaging over ~1400 steps of the warmdown trajectory instead of the last ~600 steps, smoothing the final checkpoint over more of the convergence basin.

Per-KV-head V-residual gate: expand v_res_scale from shape (1,) to (num_kv_heads=4,) so each KV head independently learns its own embedding-to-V gate strength, symmetric with the existing per-head attn_out_gate and bifurcated q_gain design.

Bifurcated K-gain [Hkv,2]: separate learnable temperature scales for RoPE dims (positional) vs NoPE dims (content) on key vectors, symmetric to the existing bifurcated q_gain [H,2]. Init 1.0 (neutral). Each of 4 KV heads independently tunes its RoPE/NoPE key sensitivity, giving full QK symmetry in positional vs content subspace control. k_gain added to CONTROL_TENSOR_NAME_PATTERNS (AdamW, not Muon NS). 8 scalar params per block × 11 blocks = 88 total params (trivial size).

Increase rope_dims 16→32: equal 50/50 split of positional (RoPE) vs content (NoPE) attention subspace in each head, matching the bifurcated q_gain design that already treats these subspaces independently (exp_156 gained -0.000476). Zero new parameters.

Halve TTT chunk size 32768→16384: 2× more adaptation opportunities across the validation sequence (more frequent per-chunk model updates) while keeping total gradient compute constant (2× chunks × half data per step). More frequent updates should better track local distribution drift in the heterogeneous FineWeb eval set.

Full-training QAT via straight-through estimator (STE): simulate per-row int6 SDClip quantization in every CastedLinear forward pass during training (clip_sigma=12.85, clip_range=31), so weights learn to cluster near int6 representable values throughout training rather than only in the final ~200 late-QAT steps; _qat_enabled disabled before GPTQ so calibration/quantization use clean fp32 weights.

Learnable per-Q-head XSA subtraction strength (xsa_alpha [num_heads=8] per block): sigmoid(5.0)≈0.993 at init (near-full XSA, preserving current behavior), lets each head independently tune how much self-projection to subtract — copying/syntactic heads can soften toward 0 while contextual heads stay near 1, adding 88 tiny scalar params with zero size impact.

Reduce matrix_clip_sigmas 12.85→5.0 for GPTQ int6: Muon-orthogonalized weight rows have max element ≈3.5σ (Gaussian theory for d=512 columns), so clip_sigmas=12.85 wastes 72% of the quantization range on non-existent tails, giving unnecessarily coarse 0.415·row_std step sizes. Reducing to 5.0 gives 2.57× finer quantization grid with essentially no actual clipping; GPTQ error-propagation compensates any residual tail weights.

GPTQ block_size 64→32: halves the within-block error-propagation window per matrix, so each column's residual affects at most 31 subsequent columns instead of 63, reducing error accumulation within blocks. Does NOT change the distribution of quantized integer values (same values, computed more accurately), so compressibility and artifact size are unchanged. More frequent Hessian-error corrections for the same total computation.

Post-GPTQ Hessian-weighted per-row scale refinement: after committing integer Q values, replace the statistics-based scale s=clip_sigma*std/clip_range with the least-squares optimal s_opt=sum(H_diag*Q*W)/sum(H_diag*Q^2) per row, strictly minimizing Hessian-weighted reconstruction error at zero artifact size cost.

Byte-weighted training CE: weight each token's loss by its UTF-8 byte length (normalized to mean=1.0) to align training objective with BPB evaluation metric — tokens with more bytes get proportionally more gradient, concentrating optimization on tokens that matter most for val_bpb.

Byte-weighted training CE (non-persistent buffer): weight each token's loss by its UTF-8 byte length (normalized to mean=1.0) to align training objective with BPB eval metric; buffer persistent=False so it's recomputed in __init__ at both train and eval deserialization — zero artifact cost.

Full-Hessian GPTQ scale refinement: replace diagonal-only per-row scale optimization with the exact s*=(W^T H Q)/(Q^T H Q) solution using the full (off-diagonal) activation Hessian, capturing input correlation structure for more accurate per-row quantization scales. Also removes broken byte-weighted CE code from previous session.

FreqGPTQ: bias GPTQ Hessian calibration toward high-frequency tokens (top-100 get sqrt(2)× activation boost → 2× H weight) so quantization error is minimized on patterns that dominate BPB; zero artifact size cost.

KV temporal shift mixing: add per-KV-head learnable tanh-bounded scalars (init 0=transparent) that blend each position's K and V with the previous position's K/V, giving lightweight local recurrence in key-value space without quantized parameters.

V normalization (RMSNorm on V before v_res addition): symmetric with existing QK normalization, making attention value vectors unit-scale before the residual is added; improves proj-layer GPTQ Hessian conditioning (more uniform activation scale → better-conditioned H = X^T X) and aligns with QKV-norm literature showing stability benefits at zero parameter cost.

Muon-VS (Variance-Scaled Muon, arxiv 2601.14603): add per-row exponential-average squared-norm tracking (beta2=0.95, the existing but unused muon_beta2 hparam) to Muon, dividing the Nesterov gradient by sqrt(EMA(||row||²)) before NS5 orthogonalization — smoother gradient scaling during training transients claims ~1.36× iteration efficiency over standard Muon.

Continuous frequency-proportional GPTQ weighting: replace binary top-100 FreqGPTQ (only 1.2% of vocab boosted) with sqrt(freq/mean_freq).clamp(0.707, 1.414) applied to all tokens — tokens with above-average frequency get smooth gradient up to 1.41× activation boost (same max as before), rare tokens are down-weighted to 0.71× instead of 1.0, covering ~20% of vocab vs top-100 while using the same 4 pre-scan calibration batches.

One-sided FreqGPTQ: change clamp(0.5, 2.0) to clamp(1.0, 2.0) so rare tokens never get penalized (weight stays ≥ 1.0) while above-average-frequency tokens still get a smooth boost up to sqrt(2)×, fixing the core failure of exp_175's symmetric scheme that downweighted rare tokens and hurt BPB.

Enable depth recurrence earlier (enable_looping_at 0.35→0.25): activates the 3-layer loop (layers 3-5 invoked 3×) at 150s instead of 210s, giving ~300 more looped training steps (~60s × ~5 steps/s) with the effectively deeper (~17-layer) architecture, at zero parameter cost.

Remove full-Hessian GPTQ scale refinement (s*=(W^T H Q)/(Q^T H Q)): exp_170 showed this feature costs +0.000157 BPB yet it persisted into exp_171's code; removing it restores the simpler per-row SDClip scale that was empirically superior. Also reverts enable_looping_at to 0.35.

Increase GPTQ calibration batches 64→96: 50% more Hessian data gives a more accurate H=X^T X estimate, reducing GPTQ reconstruction error at zero artifact size cost (Q and scale tensors are unchanged in size; only Hessian quality improves).

Learnable per-block MLP negative slope (PReLU²): replace fixed leaky_relu(0.5)^2 with a learnable neg_slope parameter (init=0.5) per block, allowing each of 11 blocks to independently discover its optimal negative-input activation strength. At init, activation is identical to current; model learns if 0.5 is optimal per layer.

Replace MLP activation from leaky_relu(0.5)² to ReLU² (pure squared ReLU, negative_slope=0→0): maximum activation sparsity (~50% zero activations) creates sparser GPTQ Hessians, concentrating calibration budget on non-zero positions for better int6 reconstruction, while ReLU² has been shown to match SwiGLU perplexity in LLMs (arXiv 2402.03804) and benefit quantized inference.

Learned per-block MLP activation mixing: add a scalar act_mix=nn.Parameter(tensor(-4.0)) per block so each block independently learns the optimal blend of relu² and leaky_relu(0.5)² via sigmoid(act_mix)*relu(h) + (1-sigmoid(act_mix))*leaky_relu(h,0.5); init at -4.0 → σ≈0.018 so training starts near exp_171's leaky_relu(0.5)² behavior while allowing blocks to learn toward relu² sparsity where it helps.

AdamW TTT (betas=(0.9,0.95), lr=0.001): replace SGD with AdamW for per-parameter adaptive learning rates during TTT; accumulated second-moment statistics across chunks act as learned per-parameter importance weights, normalizing updates by gradient variance and potentially improving adaptation quality vs. SGD's uniform momentum.

Z-loss regularization (alpha=1e-4): add 1e-4 * mean(log(sum(exp(logits)))^2) to training CE; penalizes large log-partition-function to prevent logit magnitude growth, stabilize training, and improve calibration — used in PaLM/Gemini but untried in our lineage.

LAWA (Latest Weight Averaging): replace EMA with uniform average of 5 explicit model snapshots taken at training fracs 0.85, 0.88, 0.91, 0.94, 0.97 — captures the convergent warmdown tail more precisely than the 286-step-window EMA (arxiv 2306.03241).

Last-4-blocks TTT (blocks 7-10 only) with ttt_epochs=5: restrict TTT adaptation to the last 4 blocks (lr_scale 1.2-1.5) and increase epochs 3→5 — faster backward (skip 7/11 blocks, ~60% backward savings) makes 5 epochs fit within the eval budget while focusing adaptation on semantically-rich later layers.

Remove full-Hessian GPTQ scale refinement (s*=(W^T H Q)/(Q^T H Q)): exp_178 confirmed this feature hurts BPB (exp_170 cost +0.000157 BPB); clean rebase to exp_171 then removing only these 10 lines avoids the size_blocked issue that plagued exp_178 due to accumulated workdir code.

Focal loss (gamma=0.5) during training: down-weight easy tokens (high predicted probability) and up-weight hard/rare tokens, giving the model richer gradient signal on infrequent vocabulary items that disproportionately inflate BPB.

Deeper LR warmdown: reduce min_lr from 0.05 to 0.01 (decay to 1% of peak instead of 5%), allowing the cosine warmdown's final convergence phase to reach near-zero LR as recommended by WSD / D2Z research.

Remove full-Hessian GPTQ scale refinement (s*=(W^T H Q)/(Q^T H Q)): exp_178 measured BPB=1.075363 from this edit but was size_blocked due to accumulated workdir code; rebased to clean exp_171 base, code shrinks by ~11 lines so artifact fits.

ExoFormer K residual (arxiv 2601.08131): add block[0]'s K projection of normalized x0 as a per-KV-head gated residual to all blocks' K, symmetric with the existing successful V residual; gate init sigmoid(-3.0)≈0.047, zero artifact size overhead.

Q-residual anchor (ExoFormer-style): thread block[0]'s Q projection of normalized x0 as a per-head gated residual to all blocks' Q (after RMSNorm, before RoPE+gain), symmetric with the existing V-residual that gave -0.001067 BPB. Init q_res_scale=-3.0 (sigmoid≈0.047, near-transparent). Provides raw-token query-intent anchors across all layers, especially for recurrent blocks 3-5. 88 new scalars → ~176 bytes artifact impact.

MuonTTT: replace SGD+momentum TTT optimizer with Muon-style NS-normalized momentum — accumulate gradient into momentum buffer (same as current), then apply zeropower_via_newtonschulz5 to 2D matrix buffers before the weight update; 1D params use plain heavy-ball; ns_steps=5 matches training quality.

MuonTTT with ttt_epochs=2: NS-normalized momentum TTT (LaCT arXiv 2505.23884) using zeropower_via_newtonschulz5 on 2D momentum buffers; reduce epochs 3→2 to fit eval budget (exp_195 overran by 3s with epochs=3, saving ~45s by dropping one epoch while adding ~18s NS overhead).

MuonTTT with min_dim=64 gate: NS-normalized momentum TTT applied only to large weight matrices (both dims ≥ 64), avoiding recompilation overhead from small control tensors (q_gain 8×2, resid_mix 2×512) that use AdamW in training so their shapes aren't in the NS compile cache; ttt_epochs=3 restored.

MuonTTT ns_steps=2 diagnostic: reduce NS iterations from 5 to 2 to test if NS compute is the source of the 8s eval overhead (if still 603s, overhead is from Python loop iteration, not NS; if ~598s, overhead was NS compute).

Increase warmdown_frac from 0.72 to 0.78: spend 78% of the 600s training budget in cosine LR decay (vs current 72%), giving more steps at sub-peak LR for tighter convergence as recommended by WSD/D2Z literature (arxiv 2602.06797).

Increase warmdown_frac from 0.72 to 0.75: spend 75% of training budget in cosine LR decay instead of 72%, giving more convergence time per WSD/D2Z literature while avoiding the 2.7KB size increase seen with warmdown_frac=0.78.

Increase rope_dims from 16 to 24 (37.5% of head_dim=64) to give the model richer positional encoding across more frequency pairs, with no parameter cost since q_gain bifurcation already handles the RoPE/NoPE split dynamically.

FreqGPTQ stronger boost: increase top-100 token activation multiplier from sqrt(2.0) to sqrt(3.0) (2× → 3× H weight), more aggressively minimizing GPTQ reconstruction error on the tokens that dominate BPB while keeping the same narrow top-100 coverage.
